competitors.name: Royal TS
competitors.keyDifference: Full-featured premium multi-protocol tool; RemoteConnect is a focused RDP manager with central JSON catalog and DPAPI-encrypted credentials — simpler and free
competitors.notes: Cross-platform (Win/Mac/iOS/Android). RDP, SSH, VNC, web, FTP, TeamViewer, VMware, Hyper-V. Credential management, team sharing, SSH tunneling

competitors.name: mRemoteNG
competitors.keyDifference: Stores connections in complex XML files; RemoteConnect uses centralized JSON catalog that's easy to version-control and automate with DPAPI encryption
competitors.notes: Most established open-source multi-protocol connection manager. RDP, VNC, SSH, Telnet, HTTP. Tabbed interface. Latest nightly v1.78.2 (March 2026)

competitors.name: Remote Desktop Manager (Devolutions)
competitors.keyDifference: Enterprise-grade with significant complexity and cost; RemoteConnect is lightweight with JSON-based catalog, easily scriptable without server backend
competitors.notes: Commercial centralized remote connection and credential management. 200+ integrations, role-based access, audit trails, vault storage

competitors.name: RDP Connection Manager
competitors.keyDifference: Embeds sessions in-app; RemoteConnect manages sessions via native mstsc with centralized JSON catalog for fleet-wide management
competitors.notes: Modern C# / .NET 10.0 project (2026). Microsoft RDCMan replacement. Embedded RDP/SSH sessions, DPAPI credential storage, multi-platform targets

competitors.name: MremoteGO
competitors.keyDifference: Emphasizes git-friendly YAML and 1Password integration; RemoteConnect uses centralized JSON with DPAPI encryption in the Windows security model
competitors.notes: Cross-platform Go-based connection manager. Git-friendly YAML config, 1Password integration, AES-256-GCM encryption. GUI and CLI modes

competitors.name: Microsoft RDCMan
competitors.keyDifference: Discontinued with no updates or security patches; RemoteConnect is actively maintained with modern JSON catalog and encrypted credentials
competitors.notes: Original Microsoft RDP session manager from Sysinternals. Hierarchical groups, saved credentials, multiple simultaneous sessions. No longer maintained
